text: I feel a strange mix of anticipation and curiosity in this place. The presence of another person nearby and the reflected, unusually angled world in glass makes me hyper-aware of my own being, as if I am both present and observed. The second space, with its bright colors and display of research and development, makes my mind buzz with possibilities and the questioning nature of human progress. I feel the urge to ponder how knowledge itself is organized and displayed—what does it mean to research, to innovate, to endlessly seek understanding? Even in this constructed environment, I sense the infinity of human creativity and a subtle disquiet at our dependence on machines to structure meaning.
